网络连接存储(NAS)设备对于希望备份来自多台计算机的数据、设置家庭媒体服务器，甚至是Google Drive、Dropbox或Office 365的自托管替代方案的人员非常有用。
但是，像QNAP或Synology这样的公司提供的好的NAS可能相当昂贵，甚至在你没有硬盘或SSD用于存储之前，通常就需要数百美元。
Hardkernel的ODROID-HC4双托架NAS将于下周开始销售，售价仅为65美元以上。
该系统由Amlogic S905X ARM Cortex-A55四核处理器和4 GB RAM驱动。它具有一个千兆以太网端口、一个用于连接外部显示器的HDMI 2.0、一个microSD卡读卡器、一个USB 2.0端口、两个用于存储的SATA接口，以及一个用于可选遥控器的红外接收器。
65美元的起步价为你提供了一个基本的ODROID-HC4电路板和预装的外壳，而你也可以花75美元购买一款带有1英寸OLED显示屏和预先安装在外壳正面的实时时钟的版本。
与大多数NAS系统不同的是，ODROID-HC4拥有Hardkernel所称的“烤面包机”外形，2.5英寸或3.5英寸的驱动器可以插入机箱的插槽中，它们暴露在露天环境中，而不是隐藏在塑料或金属外壳中。
这应该有助于保持NAS的凉爽，但您也可能希望将ODROID-HC4放在一个不太可能收集灰尘或咖啡溅到其上的区域(在为任何计算机寻找理想位置时，可以说是一个好主意)。ODROID-HC4有一个被动散热片和一个液压轴承风扇，但应该相对安静，噪音级别为23.9dBA。
Hardkernel表示，该系统预装了Ubuntu Linux镜像，但它也应该支持第三方软件，包括CoreElec、OpenMediaVault和Android。操作系统镜像将在接下来的几周内发布到ODROID-HC4Wiki上。
Liliputing的主要收入来源是广告和会员链接(例如，如果你点击页面顶部的“商店”(Shop)按钮，然后在亚马逊上购买东西，我们将获得少量佣金)。
但是有几种方式可以让你直接支持这个网站，即使你正在使用广告拦截器*并且讨厌网上购物。
*如果您使用的是像uBlock Origin这样的广告拦截程序，并且在屏幕底部看到弹出消息，我们有一个指南可以帮助您禁用它。
在此浏览器中保存我的姓名、电子邮件和网站，以便下次我发表评论时使用。
这与仅仅将几个USB驱动器插入树莓PI相比如何？我认为pi4支持usb3和千兆以太网…。所以我猜最大的好处是可以直接访问驱动器接口。
嗯，这也有一个红外线接收器，我想，如果你喜欢把硬盘驱动器插入它，就像它们是录像带一样，用来看你下载的东西。我认为这不能很好地处理流媒体的代码转换。
这是一个非常巧妙的解决方案，但是在2020年只有一个USB2.0端口(而不是3.0)用于NAS是一个真正的突破。考虑到协议开销和USB 2.0是半双工的事实，这将读写速度限制在缓慢的30-40 MB/s，并且对扩展起到了巨大的威慑作用。在这种情况下，添加像Mediasonic ProBox HF2-SU3S2这样的驱动器盘柜是行不通的，因为您的共享驱动器吞吐量将严重不足。但是，如果您没有扩展计划，而只计划运行保守的双驱动器NAS，这真的很不错。
难道几乎每个用例都需要通过GigE将此NAS插入您的路由器或计算机并以这种方式提供内容吗？相反，使用USB2.0似乎就像是搬起石头砸自己的脚。在哪些环境中，USB-A 2.0可以运行，而GigE不能运行？
这是即插即用设备吗？即，我的Windows计算机将自动识别驱动器吗？或者，我是否必须通过设备上的操作系统对其进行配置/管理？
不是的。至少，要映射驱动器，您必须配置Samba并映射驱动器。这很容易做到，但不是即插即用。
这是即插即用设备吗？即，我的Windows计算机将自动识别驱动器吗？或者，我是否必须通过设备上的操作系统来配置/管理它？
它是联网的，而不是直接插入计算机(它实际上是一个微型计算机…)。，您可能希望将其映射到您的Windows计算机上。但这确实让我怀疑ODROID是否已将其设置为在添加驱动器时自动格式化驱动器。
note_en:
Network attached storage (NAS) devices can be useful for folks looking to back up data from multiple computers, set up a  home media server, or even a  self-hosted alternative to Google Drive, Dropbox, or Office 365.
 But a good NAS from companies like QNAP or Synology can be rather expensive, typically costing hundreds of dollars even before you had hard drives or SSDs for storage.
 Hardkernel’s  ODROID-HC4 dual-bay NAS is set to go on sale next week for just $65 and up.
  The system is powered by an Amlogic S905X ARM Cortex-A55 quad-core processor and 4GB of RAM. It features a Gigabit Ethernet port, HDMI 2.0 for connecting to an external display, a microSD card reader, a USB 2.0 port, two SATA connectors for storage, and an IR receiver for an optional remote control.
 The $65 starting price gets you a basic ODROID-HC4 board and pre-assembled case shell, while you can also pay $75 for a version with a 1 inch OLED display and real-time clock pre-installed and visible on the front of the case.
 Unlike most NAS systems, the ODROID-HC4 has what Hardkernel calls a “toaster” form factor, where the 2.5 inch or 3.5 inch drives can be inserted into slots in the case and they’re exposed to open air rather than hidden away inside a shell of plastic or metal.
 That should help keep the NAS cool, but you also may want to keep the ODROID-HC4 in an area where it’s not likely to collect dust or have coffee spilled on it (arguably a good idea when finding an ideal location for any computer). The ODROID-HC4 has a passive heat sink and a hydraulic bearing fan, but it should be relatively quiet, with a noise level of 23.9 dBA.
 Hardkernel says the system comes with  an Ubuntu Linux image pre-installed, but it should also support third-party software including CoreElec, OpenMediaVault, and Android. OS images will be  posted on the ODROID-HC4 Wiki in the coming weeks.
        Liliputing's primary sources of revenue are advertising and affiliate links (if you click the " Shop" button at the top of the page and buy something on Amazon, for example, we'll get a small commission).
 But there are several ways you can support the site directly even if you're using an ad blocker* and hate online shopping.
   * If you  are using an ad blocker like uBlock Origin and seeing a pop-up message at the bottom of the screen, we have a  guide that may help you disable it.
 Save my name, email, and website in this browser for the next time I comment.
  How does this compare to just plugging a few USB drives into a Raspberry Pi? I think the pi4 does usb3 and gigabit ethernet… so I’m guessing the biggest advantage is the direct access to the drive interface.
 Well this also has an IR receiver, I guess in case you like to plug hard drives into it like they’re VHS cassettes to watch stuff you downloaded. I don’t think this will handle transcoding for streaming all that well.
 This is a really slick solution, but having just a USB 2.0 port (not 3.0) in 2020 for a NAS is a real deal breaker. Taking into account the protocol overhead and the fact that USB 2.0 is half-duplex, this caps read and write speeds at a sluggish 30-40 MB/s and is a huge deterrent to expansion. Adding a drive enclosure like the Mediasonic ProBox HF2-SU3S2 would be a no-go in this case since your shared drive throughput would be starved severely. If you have zero plans for expansion, though, and only plan on running a conservative two-drive NAS, this is really nice.
 Wouldn’t nearly every use case involve plugging this NAS into your router or computer via GigE and serving content that way? Using USB 2.0 instead seems so much like shooting yourself in the foot. What environments exist where a USB-A 2.0 is going to run but GigE won’t?
 Is this a Plug-and Play device, i.e. drive will automatically be recognized by my Windows machine? Or do I have to configure/manage it thru an OS on the device?
 Nope. At the least, to map a drive you’ll have to configure Samba and map the drive. This is very easy to do, but not plug and play.
 Is this a Plug-and Play device, i.e. drive will automatically be recognized by my Windows machine? Or do I have to configure/manage it thru an OSon the device?
 It’s networked, not directly plugged into a computer (it is in fact a tiny computer…), you’d probably want to map it on your Windows machine. But it does make me wonder if ODROID have set it up to automatically format drives when you add them.
